title: psephellus karduchorum
intro: Psephellus karduchorum, a flowering plant species within the daisy family, is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant has been traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Psephellus karduchorum are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits.
